steps.title: Identify the sentence type
steps.content: The structure “his voice acting work is why [blank]” makes a declarative statement explaining causation, not a question.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation and word-order rules
steps.content: Choice D correctly provides “he has become such a celebrated talent.” with a period, using the normal word order and statement punctuation needed for a declarative sentence. The other choices either invert the word order or use a question mark, which contradict the sentence's declarative nature. That matches choice D — D is correct.
